2605.17856.title: KISS - Knowledge Infrastructure for Scientific Simulation: A Scaffolding for Agentic Earth Science
2605.17856.paper_summary: Process-based simulation models encode decades of scientific understanding across the Earth sciences, yet the communities most exposed to climate risk and resource scarcity are the least able to use them. Here, we introduce knowledge infrastructure (KI), an agent-actionable scaffold that externalizes expertise into validated modelling operators, staged domain protocols, and diagnostic recovery mechanisms. Across a 3,000-trial coupled-hydrology benchmark, agents equipped with KI produced physically plausible, verifiable end-to-end simulations in up to 84% of trials, while agents without KI plateaued below 40%. KI generalizes across disciplines. We packaged its construction into a Knowledge Dissection Toolkit (KDT) that autonomously produced KI enabling end-to-end agent execution of 117 additional process-based models across 14 Earth-science domains. Across all 119 KIs, modelling decisions and failure remedies converged despite different underlying physics, showing that operational expertise is structured and extractable rather than ad hoc. Demonstrations show KI-equipped agents lowering both the access barrier between non-specialist users and process-based simulation, and the integration barrier between modelling communities. Through this scaffold, process-based science can then evolve as a living scientific commons, answerable to whoever needs to know and extendable by whoever can contribute.
2605.17856.paper_summary_zh: 基於過程的模擬模型編碼了數十年來在地球科學領域的科學理解，然而，最容易受到氣候風險和資源匱乏影響的社群卻是最無法使用這些模型的。<lb/>在這裡，我們介紹了知識基礎設施（KI），這是一種可行動的框架，將專業知識外化為經過驗證的建模運算符、階段性領域協議和診斷恢復機制。<lb/>在3000次耦合水文基準測試中，配備KI的代理在多達84%的試驗中產生了物理上合理且可驗證的端到端模擬，而沒有KI的代理則停滯在40%以下。<lb/>KI在各個學科之間具有普遍性。<lb/>我們將其構建包裝成一個知識解剖工具包（KDT），該工具包自動生成KI，使得在14個地球科學領域中能夠端到端執行117個額外的基於過程的模型。<lb/>在所有119個KI中，儘管基礎物理不同，但建模決策和失敗補救措施卻趨於一致，顯示出操作專業知識是結構化且可提取的，而非臨時的。<lb/>演示顯示，配備KI的代理降低了非專業用戶與基於過程的模擬之間的接入障礙，以及建模社群之間的整合障礙。<lb/>通過這個框架，基於過程的科學可以作為一個活的科學共同體發展，對任何需要了解的人負責，並可由任何能夠貢獻的人進行擴展。
2605.17856.author: Ziwei Li et.al.
2605.17856.authors: Ziwei Li, Liujun Zhu, Yuchen Liu, Yichen Zhao, Birk Li, Ruiqi Wu, Junliang Jin, Jianyun Zhang
